resourceCards.title: Shopify Fake Review Update: Why Honest Developers Are Excited
resourceCards.description: Ollie's reaction to Shopify's fake and incentivized review update after years of pushing for a more trustworthy App Store.
resourceCards.label: Read post

sections.title: What this blog is for
paragraphs: This blog is where I share practical notes on Shopify SEO, app quality, review trust, product updates, and the day-to-day work of building useful tools for merchants.
The focus is simple: useful context, honest opinions, and clear takeaways for store owners and app developers.

proofGallery.title: Shopify spotlight in 2026

sections.title: What a Shopify Staff Pick means
paragraphs: A Shopify Staff Pick is an app Shopify chooses to feature in the App Store. It is not a badge developers can add themselves.
If you are comparing apps, it is one more reason to take a closer look.

sections.title: Why being featured twice stands out
paragraphs: autoBlogger appeared in the spotlight in 2024 and then again in 2026. Seeing both screenshots gives merchants a clearer proof point than a single unsourced claim.
It should still sit alongside reviews, pricing, features, and the Shopify App Store listing, but it is useful context when you are deciding what to install.

sections.title: How autoBlogger helps your store
paragraphs: autoBlogger is built for stores that want to keep publishing SEO-focused blog content without turning it into another job for the team.
It helps you publish posts that support products and collections, add internal links and metadata, include FAQ content where useful, and keep content moving without a heavy editorial process.

sections.title: Proof and update log
paragraphs: This page keeps the public Staff Pick proof in one place so merchants can check the screenshots before installing.
Last updated on June 3, 2026, after the 2026 Shopify App Store spotlight screenshot was added alongside the 2024 screenshot.
items: 2024: autoBlogger shown in the Shopify App Store spotlight
2026: autoBlogger shown again in the Shopify App Store spotlight
Related proof pages: reviews, pricing, and features

faq.question: When was autoBlogger featured as a Shopify Staff Pick?
faq.answer: The screenshots on this page show autoBlogger featured in the Shopify App Store spotlight in 2024 and 2026.

faq.question: Is a Shopify Staff Pick a paid badge?
faq.answer: No. It is an editorial placement chosen by Shopify, not something developers can pay for.

faq.question: Where can I learn more before installing?
faq.answer: The features page, reviews, pricing page, and Shopify App Store listing will give you the clearest picture before you install.

sections.title: Why I am writing about Shopify fake reviews
paragraphs: I am Ollie Hermans, owner and developer of autoBlogger, autoLLMs, autoSchema, autoLock, autoShip, autoStockist, and autoBuy. I have spent years raising concerns about fake Shopify App Store reviews, incentivized reviews, and the way manipulated review signals can distort which apps merchants trust.

This has never been about knocking honest competition. It has been about fairness. Real merchants should be able to compare Shopify apps using real feedback, and developers who build properly should not be pushed down by inflated or artificial review activity.
That is why Shopify's announcement feels important. It is the first clear movement in the direction many of us in the developer community have been asking for.

sections.title: What Shopify has announced
paragraphs: Shopify has announced two updates aimed at strengthening trust in App Store reviews. The first is a dedicated App Store requirement around review incentivization. In plain English, apps should not offer, unlock, or withhold features in exchange for a merchant leaving a review.

The second is stronger fake-review detection. Shopify says it is expanding the signals used to decide whether a review is authentic, and those signals will apply to both existing reviews and new reviews.
items: Apps found incentivizing reviews can lose a significant portion of reviews.
Reviews that do not meet Shopify's authenticity bar may be unpublished.
The rollout applies to old and new Shopify App Store reviews.
Some genuine apps may lose reviews if those reviews are connected to untrustworthy reviewer activity.

sections.title: Why this feels like real movement
paragraphs: For a few years now, I have been one of the people consistently making noise about this issue, sharing examples, drumming up support, and pushing for the Shopify App Store review system to be taken seriously. A lot of honest developers have felt the same frustration.

Fake reviews and incentivized reviews create an uneven playing field. They make weaker apps look stronger than they are, make honest developers work uphill, and make merchants spend more time trying to judge which reviews are real.
So yes, I am genuinely excited to see Shopify mention stronger enforcement publicly. This is exactly the kind of movement the developer community has been hoping for.

sections.title: What honest review collection should look like
paragraphs: The right way to ask for Shopify app reviews is simple: ask real users at the right moment, use neutral language, and never offer anything in return. A review should be feedback, not a transaction.

Negative reviews should not be buried, bought away, or drowned out. They should be answered properly. Merchants notice when developers engage with feedback in a useful way, and that kind of trust is worth more than a polished review count.
items: Ask after a merchant has actually used the app.
Keep the review request neutral and optional.
Do not unlock features, discounts, credits, or support in exchange for a review.
Reply to criticism with context, fixes, or a real support path.

sections.title: What I hope happens next
paragraphs: I hope this leads to a Shopify App Store where the best apps really can win. Not the loudest apps, not the most manipulated listings, and not the apps with the most questionable review patterns.

The rollout will be worth watching closely. Review counts may change, some removals may be noisy, and Shopify will need to keep refining the signals. But if the end result is a review system merchants can trust more, that is a win for the whole ecosystem.

For my part, I will keep building the autoBlogger app family the right way: useful products, real support, honest feedback, and a long-term focus on helping Shopify merchants grow with better content, clearer data, and more trustworthy tools.

faq.question: Why is Ollie writing about Shopify App Store reviews?
faq.answer: Ollie owns and develops the autoBlogger app family and has spent years pushing publicly for Shopify to address fake and incentivized App Store reviews.

faq.question: What did Shopify announce about fake reviews?
faq.answer: Shopify announced a dedicated policy on review incentivization and stronger fake-review detection across both existing and new App Store reviews.

faq.question: Should developers ask merchants for reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, but requests should be neutral, optional, and aimed at merchants who have actually used the app.

faq.question: What should merchants do when comparing apps?
faq.answer: Look beyond the review count. Read recent reviews, check developer replies, review the app listing, and test whether the product fits your workflow.
